clusters.title: Why is the US attacking southern Iran’s civilian infrastructure?
clusters.neutral_headline: Why is the US attacking southern Iran’s civilian infrastructure
bullet_summary: Iran said it would attack "all infrastructure in the region" if President Trump follows through on his threats to attack Iranian civilian infrastructure
Reported by Al Jazeera, NPR News, BBC World and others

items.title: Why is the US attacking southern Iran’s civilian infrastructure?
items.standfirst: Analysts say the goals range from targeting Iran's supply lines to curbing its influence in the Strait of Hormuz.
items.content: Analysts say the goals range from targeting Iran's supply lines to curbing its influence in the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: U.S. strikes bridges in Iran, Tehran targets U.S. bases in the Gulf
items.standfirst: The U.S. and Iran expanded their targets in the latest round of strikes on Friday, as fighting over the control of the Strait of Hormuz reignites fears of an all-out war.
items.content: The U.S. and Iran expanded their targets in the latest round of strikes on Friday, as fighting over the control of the Strait of Hormuz reignites fears of an all-out war.(Image credit: AFP)

items.title: US launches new strikes as Iran says civilian infrastructure hit
items.standfirst: Tehran says the US strikes hit bridges, while the US boards a ship in the Strait of Hormuz.
items.content: Tehran says the US strikes hit bridges, while the US boards a ship in the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: U.S. strikes bridges around key port in Iran, expanding campaign in battle over Hormuz
items.standfirst: The United States attacked bridges and other key infrastructure in southern Iran overnight into Friday, capping nearly a week of strikes aimed at intensifying pressure on Tehran to give up control of the Strait of Hormuz.
items.content: The United States attacked bridges and other key infrastructure in southern Iran overnight into Friday, capping nearly a week of strikes aimed at intensifying pressure on Tehran to give up control of the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: US tightens grip on Iran while insisting diplomacy still possible
items.standfirst: US strikes hit transport infrastructure in Iran, including bridges, overnight between Jujly 17 and 18, Iranian media reported, marking a further escalation more than a week after fighting resumed around the strategic Strait of Hormuz. Even as the campaign intensifies, the White House insists the door to diplomacy remains open.
items.content: US strikes hit transport infrastructure in Iran, including bridges, overnight between Jujly 17 and 18, Iranian media reported, marking a further escalation more than a week after fighting resumed around the strategic Strait of Hormuz. Even as the campaign intensifies, the White House insists the door to diplomacy remains open.

items.title: US bombards Iran in sixth-straight night of attacks
items.standfirst: The US has made good on threats to bomb civilian infrastructure in Iran in its sixth-straight night of attacks.
items.content: The US has made good on threats to bomb civilian infrastructure in Iran in its sixth-straight night of attacks.

Part of President Trump's speech Thursday night is expected to touch on previously unreported alleged Chinese meddling in U

items.title: Trump’s new election push, explained
items.standfirst: US President Donald Trump used a primetime White House address to revive claims about the 2020 election.
items.content: US President Donald Trump used a primetime White House address to revive claims about the 2020 election.

items.title: Trump reviving election fraud claims to contest midterm results, say Democrats
items.standfirst: Donald Trump is reviving his long-running claims about the security of US elections. In a primetime address, the president accused China of what he called the largest election data breach in history. He also released documents he says reveal foreign interference, despite intelligence officials saying there is no evidence that foreign actors changed the outcome of a US election.
items.content: Donald Trump is reviving his long-running claims about the security of US elections. In a primetime address, the president accused China of what he called the largest election data breach in history. He also released documents he says reveal foreign interference, despite intelligence officials saying there is no evidence that foreign actors changed the outcome of a US election.

items.title: Election fraud claims and threats to media: what to know about Trump's address
items.standfirst: US President Donald Trump on Thursday revived sweeping and unsupported claims of voter fraud and Chinese meddling, firing a clear warning shot ahead of midterm elections that many expect him to dispute. What should be remembered from this speech? FRANCE 24's Andrew Hilliar looks at what was said, but also what was left unsaid, by the US president.
items.content: US President Donald Trump on Thursday revived sweeping and unsupported claims of voter fraud and Chinese meddling, firing a clear warning shot ahead of midterm elections that many expect him to dispute. What should be remembered from this speech? FRANCE 24's Andrew Hilliar looks at what was said, but also what was left unsaid, by the US president.

items.title: Trump revisits disputed claims about election security and declassifies documents
items.standfirst: In a primetime address, President Trump alleged the U.S. election system falls "catastrophically short," revisiting a topic that has drawn his attention for years — and making claims that election experts have heavily disputed.
items.content: In a primetime address, President Trump alleged the U.S. election system falls "catastrophically short," revisiting a topic that has drawn his attention for years — and making claims that election experts have heavily disputed.

items.title: Key takeaways from Donald Trump’s controversial speech on election security
items.standfirst: The president claimed China and 'deep state' actors had deceived US voters, but critics say his claims were misleading.
items.content: The president claimed China and 'deep state' actors had deceived US voters, but critics say his claims were misleading.

items.title: Watch: Trump speaks on elections, alleges Chinese access to voter data | Special Report
items.standfirst: President Trump on Thursday delivered a primetime address about election security where he railed against China. Tony Dokoupil anchored CBS News' special report.
items.content: President Trump on Thursday delivered a primetime address about election security where he railed against China. Tony Dokoupil anchored CBS News' special report.

items.title: In speech to nation, Trump declassifies intelligence claiming Chinese interference in 2020 elections
items.standfirst: US President Donald Trump declassified intelligence he said showed Chinese interference in US elections, reviving claims about election security despite intelligence findings that Beijing did not alter the 2020 vote he lost. The move reinforces Trump's push to make election integrity a central issue ahead of November's congressional midterm elections.
items.content: US President Donald Trump declassified intelligence he said showed Chinese interference in US elections, reviving claims about election security despite intelligence findings that Beijing did not alter the 2020 vote he lost. The move reinforces Trump's push to make election integrity a central issue ahead of November's congressional midterm elections.

items.title: Sen. Mark Warner slams Trump's primetime speech: "Incredible lies"

items.content: Sen. Mark Warner joins CBS News with his reaction to President Trump's primetime address on Thursday about U.S. election security.

items.title: ‘Brazen corruption’: critics denounce Trump Media plan to sell priority access to Truth Social posts
items.standfirst: Move would allow Wall Street trading firms and other institutions to potentially profit from seeing president’s posts firstDonald Trump’s media company is planning to charge for special high-speed access to Truth Social posts, including possibly his own, affecting national security and financial markets.The move announced on Thursday would allow Wall Street trading firms and other institutions to get news first from top Truth Social contributors so they could profit off subsequent moves in stocks, bonds and interest rates. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Likweli: A new monkey species discovered in the Congo Basin
clusters.neutral_headline: Watch: What we know about newly discovered monkey species
bullet_summary: Reported by Reuters Top News, BBC World, Sky News World

items.title: Likweli: A new monkey species discovered in the Congo Basin
items.standfirst: Yale researchers helped describe Likweli, a rare new Colobus monkey species from the Democratic Republic of Congo. The species was identified using physical traits, genetics, and vocalizations, and appears to have a small range in Lomami National Park and nearby forest. Researchers say the findings highlight both the region’s biodiversity and the need for conservation. [news.yale.edu]
items.content: Yale researchers helped describe Likweli, a rare new Colobus monkey species from the Democratic Republic of Congo. The species was identified using physical traits, genetics, and vocalizations, and appears to have a small range in Lomami National Park and nearby forest. Researchers say the findings highlight both the region’s biodiversity and the need for conservation. [news.yale.edu]

items.title: Italian officials handed jail terms for Genoa bridge disaster that killed 43
items.standfirst: The ex-head of Italy's motorway operator Giovanni Castellucci was handed a 12-year term over the 2018 bridge collapse.
items.content: The ex-head of Italy's motorway operator Giovanni Castellucci was handed a 12-year term over the 2018 bridge collapse.

items.title: Former boss of Italian motorways sentenced to 12 years over Genoa bridge tragedy
items.standfirst: Giovanni Castellucci among 32 people convicted over the 2018 Morandi Bridge collapse, which killed 43Thirty-two people, including the former chief executive of Italy’s motorway operator, have been convicted over the 2018 collapse of a Genoa bridge in which 43 people died.In a hushed courtroom on Thursday in the north-western Italian city, Giovanni Castellucci, a former boss of Autostrade per l’Italia, was sentenced to 12 years in prison, the highest in the case. Continue reading...

items.title: Andy Burnham declared Labour Party leader, will become U.K. prime minister
items.standfirst: Andy Burnham was officially declared leader of Britain's governing Labour Party, clearing his final hurdle to taking office as prime minister next week.
items.content: Andy Burnham was officially declared leader of Britain's governing Labour Party, clearing his final hurdle to taking office as prime minister next week.

items.title: UK’s Andy Burnham named Labour leader ahead of taking over as PM
items.standfirst: Andy Burnham was on Friday elected leader of the Labour party clearing the way for him to become the UK Prime Minister next week following the departure of Keir Starmer. Burnham has risen to leadership on a pledge to thwart the rise ‌of the populist Reform UK and said he would "give hope back" to people.
items.content: Andy Burnham was on Friday elected leader of the Labour party clearing the way for him to become the UK Prime Minister next week following the departure of Keir Starmer. Burnham has risen to leadership on a pledge to thwart the rise ‌of the populist Reform UK and said he would "give hope back" to people.

items.title: Maps show wildfire smoke forecast, air quality alerts in swath of U.S.
items.standfirst: Heavy smoke from several large wildfires blazing in Canada and Minnesota is engulfing large swaths of the Midwest and the U.S. East Coast this week.
items.content: Heavy smoke from several large wildfires blazing in Canada and Minnesota is engulfing large swaths of the Midwest and the U.S. East Coast this week.

items.title: Doctor on how concerning wildfire smoke is for health
items.standfirst: Millions of Americans across the Northeast and Midwest were under air quality alerts this week as smoke from Canadian and Minnesota wildfires settled in. At the same time, some of those same areas saw severe heat. CBS News medical correspondent Dr. Céline Gounder has more.
items.content: Millions of Americans across the Northeast and Midwest were under air quality alerts this week as smoke from Canadian and Minnesota wildfires settled in. At the same time, some of those same areas saw severe heat. CBS News medical correspondent Dr. Céline Gounder has more.

items.title: Air quality plummets in 20 US states as smoke from Canadian wildfires spreads
items.standfirst: Millions of Americans face air quality alerts from Minnesota to New York as authorities urge people to stay indoorsSign up for the Breaking News US newsletter emailTell us: have you been affected by the spread of wildfire smoke in the US and Canada?Smoke from wildfires burning in south-central Canada and parts of Minnesota is spreading across the US, prompting air quality alerts in more than 20 states with millions of Americans expected to face unhealthy air conditions this week.The smoke from the more than 180 active wildfires in northern Ontario made Chicago’s air quality the worst in the world on Thursday evening, followed by Detroit and Minneapolis, according to IQAir’s global rankings. Continue reading...
items.content: Millions of Americans face air quality alerts from Minnesota to New York as authorities urge people to stay indoorsSign up for the Breaking News US newsletter emailTell us: have you been affected by the spread of wildfire smoke in the US and Canada?Smoke from wildfires burning in south-central Canada and parts of Minnesota is spreading across the US, prompting air quality alerts in more than 20 states with millions of Americans expected to face unhealthy air conditions this week.The smoke from the more than 180 active wildfires in northern Ontario made Chicago’s air quality the worst in the world on Thursday evening, followed by Detroit and Minneapolis, according to IQAir’s global rankings. Continue reading...

items.title: Wildfires in Ontario make Toronto air quality worst in world
items.standfirst: Environment Canada has issued health warnings after sky over city turns yellowSmoke from more than 100 active wildfires in northern Ontario have made Toronto’s air quality the current worst in the world and caused yellow, smoky air in cities across the north-east US.Environment Canada issued health warnings on Wednesday after the sky over country’s largest city turned a sickly yellow and was ranked the worst in the world according to IQAir, the Swiss technology company that racks global air quality. Continue reading...
items.content: Environment Canada has issued health warnings after sky over city turns yellowSmoke from more than 100 active wildfires in northern Ontario have made Toronto’s air quality the current worst in the world and caused yellow, smoky air in cities across the north-east US.Environment Canada issued health warnings on Wednesday after the sky over country’s largest city turned a sickly yellow and was ranked the worst in the world according to IQAir, the Swiss technology company that racks global air quality. Continue reading...

clusters.title: China’s Xi says AI ‘should not be a solo performance by a single country’
clusters.neutral_headline: China’s Xi says AI ‘should not be a solo performance by a single country’

items.title: China’s Xi says AI ‘should not be a solo performance by a single country’
items.standfirst: The Chinese leader called for more international cooperation in developing the technology at a conference in Shanghai.
items.content: The Chinese leader called for more international cooperation in developing the technology at a conference in Shanghai.

items.title: China's Xi urges global cooperation on AI, warns against single-country dominance
items.standfirst: Chinese President Xi Jinping said on Friday artificial intelligence should not be dominated by any single country, calling for greater international cooperation on AI development and governance at a major conference in Shanghai. His remarks come as Chinese AI firms challenge US rivals and concerns grow over AI's military, cyber and terrorist misuse.
items.content: Chinese President Xi Jinping said on Friday artificial intelligence should not be dominated by any single country, calling for greater international cooperation on AI development and governance at a major conference in Shanghai. His remarks come as Chinese AI firms challenge US rivals and concerns grow over AI's military, cyber and terrorist misuse.

clusters.title: Japan relaxes royal succession rules - but ban on female emperors remains
clusters.neutral_headline: Japan relaxes royal succession rules - but ban on female emperors remains

items.title: Japan relaxes royal succession rules - but ban on female emperors remains
items.standfirst: The law now allows the adoption of male distant relatives aged over 15 back into the imperial family.
items.content: The law now allows the adoption of male distant relatives aged over 15 back into the imperial family.

items.title: Japan changes imperial succession law but keeps ban on female emperor
items.standfirst: Japan's parliament on Friday enacted changes to imperial succession rules, allowing male distant relatives to rejoin the imperial family and women to retain royal status after marrying commoners. The legislation, however, keeps the ban on female emperors despite opinion polls showing broad public support for allowing women to ascend the Chrysanthemum Throne.
items.content: Japan's parliament on Friday enacted changes to imperial succession rules, allowing male distant relatives to rejoin the imperial family and women to retain royal status after marrying commoners. The legislation, however, keeps the ban on female emperors despite opinion polls showing broad public support for allowing women to ascend the Chrysanthemum Throne.

clusters.title: Political crisis and protests in Ukraine as Zelenskyy defends sacking defence minister
clusters.neutral_headline: Zelensky's removal of popular defence minister sparks protests in Ukraine
bullet_summary: Reported by Guardian World, BBC World

items.title: Political crisis and protests in Ukraine as Zelenskyy defends sacking defence minister
items.standfirst: President says he had to choose ‘one side or the other’ after breakdown of relations between ministry and military leadersUkraine’s president, Volodymyr Zelenskyy, has defended his decision to dismiss the country’s popular defence minister, Mykhailo Fedorov, and confirmed reports that relations had broken down between the ministry and the country’s top army leadership.Speaking at a press conference in Kyiv with the British prime minister, Keir Starmer, Zelenskyy said there had been a “challenging dialogue” between Fedorov – widely seen as a reformist and moderniser – and the military’s commander in chief, Col Gen Oleksandr Syrskyi. Continue reading...
items.content: President says he had to choose ‘one side or the other’ after breakdown of relations between ministry and military leadersUkraine’s president, Volodymyr Zelenskyy, has defended his decision to dismiss the country’s popular defence minister, Mykhailo Fedorov, and confirmed reports that relations had broken down between the ministry and the country’s top army leadership.Speaking at a press conference in Kyiv with the British prime minister, Keir Starmer, Zelenskyy said there had been a “challenging dialogue” between Fedorov – widely seen as a reformist and moderniser – and the military’s commander in chief, Col Gen Oleksandr Syrskyi. Continue reading...

items.title: Zelensky's removal of popular defence minister sparks protests in Ukraine
items.standfirst: A rift between Fedorov and Commander-in-Chief Oleksandr Syrskyi was apparently at the heart of Zelensky's decision.
items.content: A rift between Fedorov and Commander-in-Chief Oleksandr Syrskyi was apparently at the heart of Zelensky's decision.

items.title: More than 500 feared dead after reports of two shipwrecks off Myanmar, UN says
items.standfirst: Vessels believed to have departed from Myanmar in late June, with mostly Muslim Rohingya minority onboardThe United Nations has said more than 500 people are feared dead after reports of two large shipwrecks off Myanmar since late June.The UN’s International Organization for Migration (IOM) and its refugee agency UNHCR voiced alarm in a joint statement at reports “that two boats carrying more than 500 people may have capsized off the coast of Myanmar in recent days”. Continue reading...
